summary: Practical checklist for choosing and implementing search algorithms.
examples: Scan an unsorted list of IDs to find a match.
Binary search a sorted list of SKUs.
Use a hash lookup when queries are frequent.
references: Introduction to Algorithms (Cormen et al.), Searching
diagrams: Binary search window shrinking (low, mid, high)
Decision tree: sorted vs unsorted input
content:
## Overview
Use this guide to pick the right search strategy based on data shape and constraints.

### When to use which
- Linear search: unsorted data, small collections
- Binary search: sorted data, random access
- Hash lookup: repeated queries with preprocessing

### Common pitfalls
- Off-by-one errors in binary search bounds
- Assuming data is sorted when it is not
- Ignoring duplicates and empty inputs
title: Guide 1: Searching Essentials
steps: Clarify the data shape (sorted or unsorted).
Choose linear, binary, or hash-based search.
Define the target match rule (exact, first, last).
Implement with careful bounds and test edge cases.
Measure performance on realistic input sizes.
